name: amplitude
description: Query Amplitude analytics events, segmentation, charts, and active users, and track events

envVars.name: AMPLITUDE_API_KEY
envVars.description: Amplitude project API key (basic auth username; also used by client SDKs)

envVars.name: AMPLITUDE_SECRET_KEY
envVars.description: Amplitude project secret key (basic auth password)

tools.name: List Events
tools.description: List event types visible in the project with current week totals

host.description: Amplitude analytics host: amplitude.com (US) or analytics.eu.amplitude.com (EU residency)

historicalSummary.omitted: event property details and provider-specific payload fields

tools.name: Query Event Segmentation
tools.description: Get metrics for an event over a date range (Event Segmentation chart data)

host.description: Amplitude analytics host: amplitude.com (US) or analytics.eu.amplitude.com (EU)

m.description: Metric: uniques, totals, pct_dau, or average

g.description: Property to group by, e.g. country

tools.name: Query Active Users
tools.description: Get active or new user counts over a date range

host.description: Amplitude analytics host: amplitude.com (US) or analytics.eu.amplitude.com (EU)

g.description: Property to group by, e.g. country (limit one)

tools.name: Get Chart Results
tools.description: Get JSON results from a saved Amplitude chart by chart ID

host.description: Amplitude analytics host: amplitude.com (US) or analytics.eu.amplitude.com (EU)

chartId.description: Chart ID from the chart's URL in the Amplitude web app

tools.name: Track Event
tools.description: Send events to Amplitude via the HTTP V2 ingestion API

ingestHost.description: Amplitude ingestion host: api2.amplitude.com (US) or api.eu.amplitude.com (EU)

api_key.description: Amplitude project API key — pass the same value as the AMPLITUDE_API_KEY env var (the ingestion API reads it from the body, not headers)

events.description: Events to upload; each needs event_type plus user_id (min 5 chars) or device_id, e.g. [{"user_id":"user_123","event_type":"sign_up"}]

prompts.title: Event overview
prompts.prompt: List my Amplitude event types and summarize which ones have the highest totals this week.

prompts.title: Active users trend
prompts.prompt: Show my daily active users in Amplitude for the last 30 days and describe the trend.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Amplitude API Setup

steps.title: Find your project credentials
steps.description: In Amplitude, go to Settings → Organization settings → Projects, pick your project, and copy the API Key and Secret Key. Free Starter plans include Dashboard REST API access for testing.

steps.title: Store the keys
steps.description: Add them to your .env file as AMPLITUDE_API_KEY=... and AMPLITUDE_SECRET_KEY=... — both are required because the analytics APIs use HTTP basic auth (api_key:secret_key).

steps.title: Check your data residency
steps.description: If your org uses EU data residency, pass host=analytics.eu.amplitude.com on query tools and ingestHost=api.eu.amplitude.com on Track Event. US orgs can keep the defaults.

steps.title: Verify access
steps.description: Run the List Events tool. A 401 means the key pair is wrong; a 403 can mean the keys belong to a different project or region.

notes: Keys are per-project — use a test project's keys when experimenting, since Track Event writes real analytics data

The HTTP V2 ingestion API authenticates with the project API key inside the request body, so Track Event asks for api_key as a body field (same value as AMPLITUDE_API_KEY; the secret key is never sent)
Dashboard REST API endpoints are rate limited per project — keep date ranges modest
